title: antirrhinum cornutum var. leptaleum
intro: Antirrhinum cornutum var. leptaleum, also known as the dragon flower or leopard's bane, is a plant species within the snapdragon family, characterized by its unique, tubular flowers with a distinct shape resembling a dragon's mouth. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its purported medicinal properties, including its ability to stimulate digestion, relieve respiratory issues, and act as an expectorant to clear mucus from the lungs. The health benefits associated with Antirrhinum cornutum var. leptaleum include reducing inflammation, improving circulation, and soothing respiratory problems such as coughs and bronchitis. Herbal preparations of the plant, often in the form of teas, tinctures, or infusions, can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
